source: iConnections Global Alts 2025 — The Future of AI
summary: 2025 is the year humanoid robotics becomes credibly real in public perception, with 2026 seeing consumer deployment
summary_extended: Baker predicts that 2025 will be the year humanoid robotics becomes credibly real in public perception — analogous to how ChatGPT made AI tangible for knowledge workers in 2022 — with consumer deployment following in 2026, including a specific personal prediction that he will have a Tesla Optimus robot in his home by 2026. His co-investor Gracias reinforces this framing, citing autonomous driving as a leading indicator that physical AI systems are maturing faster than consensus expects. Baker argues this shift will fundamentally alter the US productivity equation, implying humanoid robots could eventually substitute for or augment human labor at scale. The thesis is directional and medium-term, with 2025 as the credibility inflection point and 2026 as the first consumer deployment wave.
detail: Baker predicts Optimus robot in his home by 2026. Gracias expects humanoid robots to become as credibly real in 2025 as ChatGPT made AI real for knowledge workers. Autonomous driving cited as a leading indicator. Robotics will fundamentally change the US productivity equation.
confidence_evidence: Baker gives a specific personal prediction (Optimus in home by 2026) and Gracias frames 2025 as the year of the robot. Hedged with 'maybe' and 'I think' language but stated as a directional prediction.
quote_fragment: I'm going to have a humanoid robot the Optimus robot in my house in 2026

ticker_audit.reasoning: Baker specifically names the Optimus robot, which is Tesla's humanoid robot product, and predicts having one in his home by 2026. Tesla is the direct subject of the consumer humanoid deployment claim.
